item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: While it is well written… Nothing really happens. An alternate reality… Had a big bad thing happen? I don't know, it doesn't say. A sinkhole opened, a disease got around, but other than that nothing happens. A "reality failure" Occurred? Well what the hell does that mean? What are it's affects? Maybe if I could, I dunno, READ what the German said, I would be able to vote. For now I'm neutral.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
You made me feel empathy towards a Nazi… I tought this would never happen… that is good writing
+1

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: I absolutely love apocalyptic/horror AltHist, too bad it doesn't show up much outside of the Foundation.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: Did I just read an article about Nazi Zombies? On this fine quality wiki? And ENJOY it? _1 for sheer trickery.

item.title: Re: Staff Post - Open
item.content:encoded: I like both links, but I think the 1322 one is stronger.

item.title: Re: Staff Post - Open
item.content:encoded:
Huh.

Well if that option is not up to snuff, perhaps we should try this one on for size:

At this time, communications are restricted to receiving and recording the signal broadcast by SCP-1142-1 from Dm-AX/15487-A; attempts to initiate contact with or send a signal to Dm-AX/15487-A have proven ineffectual. Following the events of Foundation communication with SCP-1322-A, further research into initiating contact with SCP-1142-1 or responding to its transmissions has been suspended.

SCP-1322 has definitely shown the Foundation what can potentially happen when you meddle in a parallel universe, and I'm sure they would not be so quick to meddle again, given that they may bork another universe or lead to events that bork ours.

item.title: Re: Staff Post - Open
item.content:encoded:
…I don't think 908 was what you meant to link. 908 is the rock that's in several places at once.

E: Nevermind, I see it now, but the connection is flimsy at best. I'm not a fan of it.

item.title: Staff Post - Open
item.content:encoded:
Timer Expired. Edits made as discussed below. Author may revert at any time.

The WikiWalk team is proposing edits to this page. All proposed edits will be in a collapsible below this post, and after 7 days if there are no serious objections to the edits, the feedback received will be taken into account as the edits are made. If you have questions not directly related to the article about this project, please pm a member of the WikiWalk Team

+ Show Block
- Hide Block

Both this article, and SCP-908 have similar vibes in how they play into the act of dimension jumping, and things from other worlds leaking into our own. Both also feature 20th century German weapons that have modernized capabilities, helping to tie the link a little closer together. Additionally, a Wikiwalk link has already been established from 908 to here, so a back link couldn't hurt. As such, I am recommending the following edits:

Changing this:

SCP-1142 is a Goliath tracked mine (original designation Leichter Ladungsträger Goliath (Sd.Kfz. 303b)) as used by the German Wehrmacht during the Second World War. SCP-1142 shows no signs of aging and although originally designed to carry an explosive charge, contains a heavily modified Kw.E.a Wehrmacht radio receiver within its housing. The modified components are of a design and complexity inconsistent with the level of German technology during the middle of the twentieth century. SCP-1142 relies on a gasoline engine for propulsion and carries an early model thermal battery for the operation of the receiver.

To this:

SCP-1142 is a Goliath tracked mine (original designation Leichter Ladungsträger Goliath (Sd.Kfz. 303b)) as used by the German Wehrmacht during the Second World War. SCP-1142 shows no signs of aging and although originally designed to carry an explosive charge, contains a heavily modified Kw.E.a Wehrmacht radio receiver within its housing. The modified components are of a design and complexity inconsistent with the level of German technology during the middle of the twentieth century1. SCP-1142 relies on a gasoline engine for propulsion and carries an early model thermal battery for the operation of the receiver.

If anyone has other/better examples that could be used here please leave a comment saying so!

Footnotes
1. A connection to SCP-908 has been theorized due to similar designs between SCP-1142's internal equipment and recovered materials from SCP-908.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: I love this, would be really great to see it expanded further in Tale form but as far as the SCP report itself goes there's pretty much the perfect amount there, really well-done piece

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: I live in Lublin province of Poland and 16km sinkholes aren't even the weirdest things around.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: This genuinely interests me and I would kill to know more about what's going on over there. A really well thought and written scip. Upvoted

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: The logs make this. Like, remove the logs, and it's a curiosity, but the logs add a sort of horror, and, I hate to admit this but, this thing manages to evoke sympathy for Nazis. I very much like this and the tale whose discussion linked me here. Good job.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: Like this, though the transmissions are almost painful to read due to formatting.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: Upon reading this one more, and other things, I find that I don't want to downvote it anymore. Neutral voted instead.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: Headcanon: SCP-108's nose is a portal to the alternate reality that SCP-1142 comes from.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: Just a random guess, but would they happen to be trying to isolate SCP-8900-EX since both would fit in around the same time?

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: But they are Nazi Chinchillas!
My first+100. Yay?

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: I have a a hard time isolating exactly what, but something here grabs me. There's this air of mystery about it that delights me. Crayne, i would love to see a story of what you have in mind for what's happening on the other side. Curse my alternate history fetish!

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
Once again, I find myself wishing I could upvote comments. Eh, at least I can upvote articles.

+1

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: Still waiting for that tale, Anaxagoras. ;)

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
Basically zerg-rushing Hitler… brilliant.
There is this joke, that the Titanic sank because of overweight. The additional weight consists of timetravellers, trying to find out why it sank.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: Write this into a tale. Seriously, it's that neato.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: I love that interpretation. It's not what I had in mind, and TV Tropes doesn't get it right either, but it's an awesome take.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: That… makes a lot of sense. I'm not sure that I would personally use it as headcanon for this SCP, but it certainly is a strong interpretation.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: Hey, so I just saw this, and I have a theory for what's really going on. You know how time travelers want to go back in time and stop the Axis? This is the timeline they all go to. Germany in this is being subjected to the wrath of about a dozen time travelers, operating independently, and using phenomenally advanced future technology against the Germans.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
I was more reminded of Resistance: Fall of Man, but I should stop being terrible and referencing other media.

I agree with Tab, clean up the radio messages a bit more, though that would most likely lessen the impact of the "help" at the end.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
Upvote, definitely, but with one nitpick in the formatting department:

In the radio transmissions, asterisks and lack of spacing in between words were used to denote the unintelligible parts. I can't decide whether the lack of readability there adds a properly… well, unintelligible effect on the text, or if said effect wouldn't be destroyed if I went in and added brackets and spacing, which, while making the text a little easier on the eyes, also seems more scientific-looking and less casual/SMS-ish than asterisks.

Thoughts, anyone?

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: i enjoyed this one and upvoted, but theres one thing bothering me. how is the reciever recieving anything at all? is the portal in which the alternate timeline germans still open somehow or did they also devolope a way to send information through realities without an open portal?

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: Aha. I just thought I'd ask, as the tone and such are quite similar. If you had been inspired, that would certainly be no bad thing.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
Help

Man, I like this! Exploring alternate realities is something I love reading about since that dimensions-jumping guy I cannot remember the number of. While the topic of WW2 Germany had been used a lot, this was a well written take. +1

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: Sorry I missed this comment. This is actually the first time I've heard of The Life Eaters, so no. I think this started life as a mobile temporal displacement portal used by the Nazis to escape the fall of Berlin with a visual of Wehrmacht halftracks rolling out of some barn in rural Minnesota.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: I have to ask, was this inspired at all by The Life Eaters?

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: I enjoyed it enough to +1, but I think the transmissions, despite being frequently unintelligible, are far too easy to understand. There should be more clear segments which don't offer useful/interesting text.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: And now I can upvote in good conscience. I thank you, and the chinchillas thank you.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: Fixed the improper use of the term dimension. Blame the SF/horror reader in me.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: Do you mean refer to the Italian and French army in their mothertongue or refer to the German bomb disposal unit as "the German guys who defuse bombs"?

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: Well, it's certainly all about the Axis.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: I weep for all the dead extradimensional chinchillas.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: This is why you don't give the Wehrmacht a Shoggoth.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: "Another timeline" works. "Another reality" works. "Another universe" works. "Another location in two-dimensional time" works. "Another dimension" implies that this other world is aligned on the Q axis instead of or in addition to X, Y, Z, and/or T.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: I like alternate timeline stuff…but this seems kind of run-of-the-mill, and it doesn't really grab me. The fact that every other word in the log was unintelligible made it annoying to read.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: Oh, I know, I was just teasing.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
Help

+1

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: there are only 11 dimensions but there are an infinite number of potential timelines in the 4th dimension if you can move sideways through time. People often get that confused.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
The word is used that way frequently in some science fiction, but that doesn't make it any less inacurate, particularly in a (mock) scientific document. A dimension for our purposes refers to height, width, depth, and so forth. It does not refer to other universes / timelines / whatever. Though I may be butchering the physics, I believe it's accurate to say that a reality has dimensions; it is not a dimension itself.

My apologies if I'm coming on strong on this point, but this is a serious peeve of mine.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: And if you did that you'd have my upvote : P

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: I do so love alternate timeline stuff. +1

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: I dig it.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: You, sir, have a point there (on the diameter/radius thing).

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: Thanks everyone for your feedback. On the whole 'dimensional' thing, I'm open to suggestions. Anyone have a good technical term to describe this sort of thing. Alternate reality seems a bit too mainstream though I suppose it'd do. Also, I need something for interdimensional. Inter-reality just doesn't cut it…

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: I liked it overall, the very last transmission is - for once - the thing that actually made me go ahead and scroll up to the +1. I was probably gonna anyway, but that sealed it.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: NSDAP - National Socialist German Workers' Party, to cite proper name

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: They're members of the German National Socialist Party. They may not be Nazis, but they are Nazis.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: I am sick to death of the "Hitler and the Occult" genre, so it takes a lot for me to like a concept from that mold. This was very well written though, and I like the transmissions. Upvoted, and good work.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
You nearly made me feel bad for a literal Nazi, which is impressive.

*Ben Grimm voice on* It's NITPICKING TIME! *Ben Grimm voice off*

There are various interpretations of what's considered a "Nazi" when you refer to German military personnel during WWII. There are some people who use the word "Nazi" interchangeably with "German" for the period, but that's IMHO way too broad a view; many in the Wehrmacht did not share the views of the leadership, and were fighting because they were conscripted or out of a general desire to protect their homeland.

When it comes to military command positions, it gets even thornier. Many of the German High Command were there prior to the Third Reich, and in fact had a great distaste for the politics of the era, but served out of perceived duty. Other were enthusiastic and commanded SS divisions and such. I'm not sure what the particulatrs of our skip's situation are, but I don't know if he's automatically a "Nazi" as such.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
reminds me of SCP-1639. both are good.

and yeah, this isn't from another dimension, it's from another reality.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: "There are hundreds of thousands of dimensions separate from our own, each of these alternate dimensions connect to thousands more. Can you comprehend that? There is an infinite amount of dimensions around us, in one dimension you don't exist, in another dimension you're a lion tamer, and in another dimension you could be a chinchilla."

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
You nearly made me feel bad for a literal Nazi, which is impressive. Unfortunately, that is NOT what "dimension" means. When discussing alternate universes / timelines / what-have-you, please do not call them dimensions. Hundreds of chinchillas die every time you do.

EDIT: bloody phone typos…

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
At the very least, using brackets would make it a lot easier to read — as it is it was kind of hard to tell where the real words were. Which may have been the visual equivalent of listening to it, who knows!

Anyway, I liked it too. I wanna know (imagine, I mean) what caused the sinkhole. Although, thinking on that — and this is really picky — subsidence is usually measured in diameter rather than radius. Probably because nobody's climbing into the center of the thing to find where a radius would start. ;)
